claim_boundary: The theorem excludes globally accurate use of the explicit half-turn-invariant Q=0 state. It does not exclude Q=0 states with trivial stabilizer, stabilizers approaching the identity, or cross-spin states whose global orbit is asymptotically free.
general_theorem: If U(h) rho U(h)^*=rho and h has principal rotation angle alpha, then every Haar-prior SO(3) orientation protocol with chordal cost obeys R_ref>=sin^2(alpha/4).
goal: Global Orientation Risk From State Stabilizers

spin_two_anticoherent_example.consequence: The explicit Q=0 state has full-rank local rotational QFI but cannot achieve global Haar-prior orientation risk below 1/2.
